---
title: "values and goals reset"
format: html
---

## Values and Goals Reset

Recovery is easier to sustain when daily choices connect to personal values. During stressful periods, people often drift from values to short-term relief.

A values reset helps you realign quickly: clarify what matters, choose one concrete behavior, and review daily.

Meaningful direction protects recovery. Stressful transitions or role demands can increase risk if goals are unclear or daily actions drift away from personal values.

Clarifying values and translating them into concrete steps improves focus, persistence, and self-respect. This creates a stronger foundation for long-term behavior change.

::: callout
### Reflection Questions
1. What kind of person do I want to be in recovery?
2. Which value has felt hardest to live this week?
3. What one action today would move me closer to that value?
:::

| Value | Behavior that shows this value | When I will do it |
|---|---|---|
| Health | | |
| Honesty | | |
| Responsibility | | |
| Connection | | |
